LAW AND MOTION, 302, AS TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, DEFENDANT CCSF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AS TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RACE DISCRIMINATION AND DENIED AS TO HIS SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION. DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING NO EVIDENCE OF A DISCRIMINATORY MOTIVE BY DEFENDANT AND PLAINTIFF DID NOT PRESENT ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE ON THAT POINT. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES AS TO ALL THE ELEMENTS OF A PRIMA FACIE CASE FOR RETALIATION, WHETHER DEFENDANT TOOK THE ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS TAKEN AGAINST PLAINTIFF AFTER HE FILED HIS 2013 DFEH COMPLAINT FOR LEGITIMATE NON-RETALIATORY REASONS AND WHETHER THOSE REASONS ARE PRETEXTUAL. COUNSEL SHALL MEET AND CONFER ON THE LANGUAGE OF A PROPOSED ORDER AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE VIA E-MAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. GREGORY P. BROCK, ESQ. of Brock Law Office, Tel No. 510-841-1171 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Deputy City Attorney LISA BERKOWITZ, ESQ. of City and County of San Francisco Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. 415-554-3825 appearing for the Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, Defendant CCSF's motion for summary judgment is granted as to plaintiff's first cause of action for race discrimination and denied as to his second cause of action for retaliation. Defendant satisfied its burden of showing no evidence of a discriminatory motive by defendant and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue on that point. There are triable issues as to all the elements of a prima facie case for retaliation, whether defendant took the adverse employment actions taken against plaintiff after he filed his 2013 DFEH complaint for legitimate non-retaliatory reasons and whether those reasons are pretextual. Counsel shall meet and confer on the language of a proposed order and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature via e-mail to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
